page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.sn2.xlarge delivers modest performance across most workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests falls into the bottom 25%, and Geekbench single-core scores are in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read-only performance for both small and large blocks, though large block write bandwidth achieves an average rating. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, perform poorly, placing in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested instances.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.sn2.xlarge offers a dedicated hardware profile with a standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but its performance-per-resource ratio is constrained by its older processor architecture. Tradeoffs include weak database operation speeds and low static web serving throughput, both ranking in the bottom 25%. However, the instance maintains average performance for small LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation with a 135M model. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, basic utility applications, and lightweight development environments that require dedicated CPU cores but do not demand high-throughput processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Running on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. While large block write memory bandwidth and small-model LLM inference achieve average ratings, database operations and static web serving throughput remain weak. Consequently, the ecs.sn2.xlarge is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight utility services, and development environments where dedicated CPU cores are required but high computational throughput is not critical.

Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage.
Average performance for small-model LLM inference tasks.
